name: Jhal Fireze- Beef with Onion and Green Pepper - India
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces cooked, boneless roast beef or lamb, cut into 1/4-inch slivers
1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper, or to taste
1/2 teaspoon black or yellow mustard seeds
1 small green bell pepper, cut into 1/4-inch slivers
1 small onion, cut into fine half rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut cooked meat into 1/4-inch wide slices.
2. Stack the slices together and cut them into 1/4-inch slivers.
3. Combine black pepper, cayenne, cumin, coriander, turmeric, vinegar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and water in a small cup. Mix well and set aside.
4. Heat vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
5. When the oil is hot, add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, and optional fenugreek seeds.
6. As soon as the mustard seeds begin to pop, add the green bell pepper and onion.
7. Stir-fry until the onions have browned quite a bit and most of the vegetable mass has reduced.
8. Sprinkle about 1/8 teaspoon salt over the top and stir.
9. Add the meat and spice mixture from the cup.
10. Stir rapidly for a minute or so until the meat has heated through.
11. Add Worcestershire sauce and stir to mix.
